text:
From the all-American flair of Team USA to the grace and elegance of Malaysia’s “The Malaya” collection, the athletes’ apparel did more than just showcase their national pride – it told captivating stories of their diverse cultures. We spotlight the best Olympic team outfits from the Paris 2024 Opening Ceremony, celebrating the creativity and cultural significance behind each look.



The kick-off extravaganza of theParis 2024 Olympicswas a tribute to the rich tapestry of cultures worldwide. A riot of colour and style took centre stage as each country’s fashion choices became a lively homage to its unique heritage.

This year’s event was a powerful testament to fashion’s remarkable ability to unite cultures and honour traditions on the grandest stage.

Let’s take a closer look at the fabulous team outfits that stole the spotlight during this unforgettable ceremony.





A post shared by Polo Ralph Lauren (@poloralphlauren)



Ralph Lauren designed Team USA’s outfits, which featured a classic and patriotic look with striped shirts, denim, and suit jackets in red, white, and navy blue.The designs are aimed to evoke a sense of American patriotism with a classic, Ivy League-inspired look.







A post shared by Michel&Amazonka (@michelamazonka)



Designed by Michel Choigaalaa and Amazonka Choigaalaa, Mongolia’s outfits included long white pleated skirts, embroidered vests, and traditional deels. The designs included celestial drawings and animal symbols reflecting Mongolian folklore. This blend of tradition and modernity showcases Mongolia’s rich cultural heritage on the global stage.







A post shared by Olympic Council of Malaysia (@olympicmas)



Malaysia’s athletes are set to make a fashion statement like never before, thanks to the popular designer Rizman Ruzaini. The dazzling collection, aptly named “The Malaya,” draws inspiration from traditional Malay attire, featuring eye-catching ensembles such as the Baju Melayu Teluk Belanga paired with a songkok and sampin for men, and the Baju Kurung with headscarves for women. The designs, in gold, olive green, and brown, pay homage to Malaysian heritage and symbolise aspirations for Olympic gold​







A post shared by JUST IN XX (@justinxx_official)



Justin Chou designed Chinese Taipei’s uniforms. It showcased hand-crafted flowers and elements representing the region’s culture, art, and craftsmanship, emphasising sustainability and cultural pride.







A post shared by Canadian Olympic Team (@teamcanada)



Lululemon created bold red-on-red uniforms for Canada, incorporating sustainable materials and subtle references to the country’s national colours and symbols. The attire included jackets, pants, and skirts made from sustainable materials, with subtle nods to Canada’s national colours and symbols.







A post shared by LeagueFits (@leaguefits)



Sudan’s athletes rocked their signature black suits adorned with traditional patterns, eye-catching fabrics, and meaningful symbols that proudly showcased the nation’s rich cultural heritage.







A post shared by Stella Jean (@stellajean_sj_)



Taking a cue from the rich tapestry of traditional Haitian clothing, Haiti’s uniforms were a delightful fusion of vivid prints and patterns that celebrated the nation’s rich artistic and cultural heritage. These outfits were more than just clothing; they were vibrant expressions of Haiti’s indomitable spirit and unwavering strength.







A post shared by Bermuda Olympic Association (@nocbermuda)



Bermuda’s team showcased their attire, which included Bermuda shorts in national colours, blazers, and ties. This unique style is a nod to the country’s tradition and laid-back island culture.



These costumes not only highlighted the distinctive identities of each nation but also brought colour and variety to theParis 2024 Olympic opening ceremony.

title: Our Top 8 Olympic Team Outfits from the Paris Opening Ceremony- Harper's BAZAAR Malaysia
author: Fareena IIisya Khan
description: Uncover the vibrant outfits of the Olympic 2024 teams and explore how fashion became a powerful expression of cultural heritage.
